content_en:
On certain occasions, it is lawful to show approval and hide one’s denial like in the case of he who is forced to say words of disbelief. What is meant here is that it is inevitable that everyone will face affliction and harm. There is no way to be saved from affliction. That is why Allah, the Exalted mentioned this point in several places saying that people have to be afflicted and that affliction can be by means of happiness and misfortune. Man_ will be afflicted with what pleases and harms him, so he needs to be patient and praise Allah.

Allah, the Almighty says, 4 nee gf a Fe wee gs 4 Sus SST asks Oy MI Je u chs bl >

which means, "Verily! We have made that which is on earth as an adornment for it, in order that We may test them (mankind) as to which of them are best in deeds. [l.e. those who do good deeds in the most perfect manner, that means to do them (deeds) totally for Aliah's sake and in accordance to the legal ways of the Prophet]." (Al-Kahf, 18:7)

And He, the Almighty says,

4 ope a EG ATE gialis... >

which means, "We tried them with good (blessings) and evil (calamities) in order that they might turn (to Allah's Obedience)."

And He, the Almighty says,

Zs . th eboee ee BP ae ee oe ete Bae iit OS 68 ot oil Sy BH UH SG Let 58 ote eke ee Bee

which means, "Then if there comes to you guidance from Me, then whoever follows My Guidance shall neither go astray, nor fall into distress and misery. "But whosoever turns away from My Reminder (i.e. neither believes in this Qur'an nor acts on its orders, etc.) verily, for him is a life of hardship, and We shall raise him up blind on the Day of Resurrection." (Taha, 20:123124)

And He, the Almighty says,

which means, "Do you think that you will enter Paradise before Allah tests those of you who fought (in His Cause) and (also) tests those who are As-Sdbirin (the patient ones, etc.)?" (Al-

This verse is in Al-'Imran and He, the Almighty says before that in chapter Al-Baqarah,

AL-FAWA'ID ; A Collection of Wise Syaings * 349

“ fe gee SPL ge al te IF ge ie 8

4a lye 8 Waele Oe ge

which means, "Or think you that you will enter Paradise without such (trials) as came to those who passed away before you? They were afflicted with severe poverty and ailments and were so shaken that even the Messenger and those who believed along with him said, “When (will come) the Help of Allah?" Yes! Certainly, the Help of Allah is near!" (Al-Baqarah, 2:214)

The soul will never become pious and purified except through undergoing afflictions. It is the same as gold that can never be pure except after removing all the base metals in it. The soul is ignorant and unjust and this is the origin of the evil that confronts the servant. Aliah, the Almighty says,

which means, "Whatever of good reaches you, is from Allah, but whatever of evil befalls you, is from yourself." (An-Nisa’, 4:79)

And He, the Almighty says,

which means, "(What is the matter with you?) When a single disaster smites you, although you smote (your enemies) with one twice as great, you say: "From where does this come to us?" Say (to them), "It is from yourselves (because of your evil deeds)." (Ai-Imran, 3:165)

350 * AL-FAWA'ID: A Collection of Wise Sayings

And He, the Almighty says,

which means, "And whatever of misfortune befalls you, it is because of what your hands have earned. And He pardons much." (Ash-Shara, 42:30)

And He, the Almighty says,

Begs oe é 2

which means, "That is so because Allah will never change a grace which He has bestowed on a people until they change what is in their ownselves." (Al-Anfal, 8:53)

And He, the Almighty says, 4B 5 ct 95 ot nd “id 3 Sb Kee agi dl sigh 13)... >

which means, "But when Allah wills a people's punishment, there can be no turning back of it, and they will find besides Him no protector." (Ar-Ra'd, 13:11)

And Allah mentions the punishments of all the nations from the time of Adam (Adam, peace be upon him) until late in time. And during all that He mentioned how they wronged themselves and Allah was not unjust to them. The first to confess that is our parents; Allah says that they said,

oe Pr or ea ee 6 a ne

which means, "Our Lord! We have wronged ourselves. If You forgive us not, and bestow not upon us Your Mercy, we shall certainly be of the losers.” (Al-A’raf, 7:23)

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 351

And Allah says to Satan,

which means, "That | will fill Hell with you [/blis (Satan)] and those of them (mankind) that follow you, together." (Sad, 38:85)

And Allah says that Satan said,

which means, "O my Lord! Because you misled me, | shall indeed adorn the path of error for them (mankind) on the earth, and | shall mislead them all. "Except Your chosen, (guided) Slaves among them." (Al-Hijr, 15:39-40)

And Allah, the Almighty says, Bp gill je UT ys YELL 3 ie 5 coatis Sy}

which means, "Certainly, you shall have no authority over My slaves, except those who follow you of the Ghawin (Mushrikan and those who go astray, criminals, polytheists, and evil-doers,

Our pious predecessors acknowledge this fact like the sayings of Abu Bakr, ‘Umar, and Ibn Mas'id: | will say my opinion concerning this matter, if it is right, it is granted by Allah and if it iS wrong it is from me.

And in the divine hadith, Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) narrated that the Messenger (peace be upon him) said that Allah said, "My servants, these for you | shall reward you for them, so he whose deeds of yours which | am recording finds

good, should praise Allah and he who does not find that should not blame anyone but his own self."*!

It was mentioned in the authentic hadith that is the hadith of Sayyid Al-Istighfar (The best supplication for asking forgiveness), that the servant should say, “Allahumma Anta Rabby, a ildha ill4 anta, Khalagtany wa-ana ‘abduka, wa-ana ‘ala ‘ahdika wa-wa‘dika mastata'tu, a‘idhu bika min sharri ma sanatu, Abd’u laka Bini'matika ‘alayya, wa-abi’u Bithanby, Fagfir ly, fa’innahu |@ yaghtirudh-dhundba illa anta. (O Allah, you are my Lord, there is no god but You. You created me | am Your servant, and | am always trying to fulfill my promise and covenant with You as much as | can. | seek refuge with You from the evil that | have done. | acknowledge Your Blessings upon me and | acknowledge my sins. | beseech You to forgive me, for none forgives sins but You.) If somebody recites it during the day with firm faith in it, and dies on the same day before the evening, he will be from the people of Paradise; and if somebody recites it at night with firm faith in it, and dies before the morning, he will be from the people of Paradise."

And it was narrated by Abu Bakr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) taught him what to recite during the day and the night and when he is about to sleep, "Allahumma Fatir is-samawat wal-ard, ‘alimi alghaibi wash-shahadah, rabi kuli shai'in wa malikahi, ashhadu an 1a ilaha ila anta, a‘ddhu bika min sharri nafsy wa sharri ashshaitan wa shirkihi, wa an agtarif ‘ala nafsy sd’an aw ajurahu ila muslim. (O Allah! Creator of the Heavens and the Earth. The All-Knower of the unseen and the seen. The Lord of everything

5" Recorded by Muslim, book of Virtue, good manners and joining the ties of kinship.

82 Recorded by Al-Bukhary, book of invocations, chapter on the best of supplications, no.6306.

AL-FAWA'ID : A Collection of Wise Syaings * 353

which means, “So be patient (O Muhammad). Verily, the Promise of Allah is true, and let not those who have no certainty

Recorded by Ibn Majah, no.88, Ahmad in his Musnag, vol.4, no.419, and ‘Abd bin Hamid, no.535, narrated by Ibn Masa and it is Marfd' (traceable),

354 * AL-FAWA'D: A Collection of Wise Sayings

of faith, discourage you from conveying Allah's Message (which you are obliged to convey). (Ar-Rim, 30:60)

It is said that he who is stable and whose belief is stable _ will have true belief and faith within his heart both through deeds and knowledge. The knowledge of a servant may be good but his soul cannot be patient during times of hardship, so he goes astray.
content_id:
Pada situasi-situasi tertentu, diperbolehkan untuk menunjukkan persetujuan dan menyembunyikan penolakan seseorang, seperti dalam kasus orang yang dipaksa mengucapkan kata-kata kekufuran. Yang dimaksud di sini adalah bahwa setiap orang pasti akan menghadapi ujian dan penderitaan. Tidak ada cara untuk terhindar dari ujian. Itulah sebabnya Allah Ta'ala menyebutkan hal ini di beberapa tempat, bahwa manusia pasti akan diuji dan bahwa ujian itu bisa berupa kebahagiaan maupun kesengsaraan. Manusia akan diuji dengan apa yang menyenangkan dan menyakitinya, sehingga ia perlu bersabar dan memuji Allah.

Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Sesungguhnya Kami telah menjadikan apa yang ada di bumi sebagai perhiasan baginya, agar Kami menguji mereka (manusia), siapakah di antara mereka yang terbaik amalnya. [Yaitu mereka yang melakukan amal kebaikan dengan cara yang paling sempurna, yang berarti melakukannya semata-mata karena Allah dan sesuai dengan cara-cara yang disyariatkan oleh Nabi]." (Al-Kahf, 18:7)

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Kami menguji mereka dengan kebaikan (nikmat) dan keburukan (musibah) agar mereka kembali (kepada ketaatan kepada Allah)."

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Maka jika datang kepadamu petunjuk dari-Ku, barangsiapa yang mengikuti petunjuk-Ku, ia tidak akan sesat dan tidak akan celaka. Tetapi barangsiapa yang berpaling dari peringatan-Ku (yaitu tidak beriman kepada Al-Quran ini dan tidak mengamalkan perintah-perintahnya), maka sesungguhnya baginya penghidupan yang sempit, dan Kami akan mengumpulkannya pada hari kiamat dalam keadaan buta." (Taha, 20:123-124)

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Apakah kamu mengira bahwa kamu akan masuk surga, padahal belum nyata bagi Allah orang-orang yang berjihad (di jalan-Nya) di antara kamu dan belum nyata orang-orang yang sabar?" (Ali

Ayat ini terdapat dalam surah Ali 'Imran, dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman sebelumnya dalam surah Al-Baqarah,

AL-FAWA'ID; Kumpulan Hikmah * 349

yang artinya, "Apakah kamu mengira bahwa kamu akan masuk surga, padahal belum datang kepadamu (cobaan) sebagaimana halnya orang-orang terdahulu sebelum kamu? Mereka ditimpa oleh malapetaka dan kesengsaraan, serta digoncangkan (dengan bermacam-macam cobaan) sehingga berkatalah Rasul dan orang-orang yang beriman bersamanya, 'Bilakah datangnya pertolongan Allah?' Ingatlah, sesungguhnya pertolongan Allah itu amat dekat!" (Al-Baqarah, 2:214)

Jiwa tidak akan pernah menjadi saleh dan bersih kecuali dengan menjalani ujian-ujian. Hal ini sama seperti emas yang tidak akan pernah murni kecuali setelah membuang semua logam dasar di dalamnya. Jiwa itu bodoh dan zalim, dan inilah asal mula kejahatan yang menimpa seorang hamba. Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Apa saja nikmat yang kamu peroleh adalah dari Allah, dan apa saja bencana yang menimpamu, maka dari (kesalahan) dirimu sendiri." (An-Nisa', 4:79)

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "(Mengapa kamu ditimpa musibah?) Ketika satu musibah menimpa kamu, padahal kamu telah menimpakan kekalahan dua kali lipat kepada musuh-musuhmu, kamu berkata, 'Dari mana datangnya ini?' Katakanlah kepada mereka, 'Itu dari diri kamu sendiri (karena perbuatan buruk kamu).'" (Ali 'Imran, 3:165)

350 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Hikmah

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Dan apa saja musibah yang menimpa kamu, maka adalah disebabkan oleh perbuatan tanganmu sendiri. Dan Dia memaafkan sebagian besar (dari kesalahan-kesalahanmu)." (Asy-Syura, 42:30)

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Yang demikian itu adalah karena sesungguhnya Allah tidak akan mengubah suatu nikmat yang telah diberikan-Nya kepada suatu kaum hingga kaum itu mengubah apa yang ada pada diri mereka sendiri." (Al-Anfal, 8:53)

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Tetapi apabila Allah menghendaki hukuman atas suatu kaum, maka tidak ada yang dapat menolaknya, dan tidak ada pelindung bagi mereka selain Dia." (Ar-Ra'd, 13:11)

Dan Allah menyebutkan hukuman-hukuman terhadap semua umat sejak zaman Adam (Adam, 'alaihis salam) hingga akhir zaman. Dan dalam semua itu, Dia menyebutkan bagaimana mereka menzalimi diri mereka sendiri, dan Allah tidaklah berlaku zalim terhadap mereka. Yang pertama kali mengakui hal tersebut adalah kedua orang tua kita; Allah berfirman bahwa mereka berkata,

yang artinya, "Ya Tuhan kami, kami telah menganiaya diri kami sendiri. Jika Engkau tidak mengampuni kami dan tidak merahmati kami, niscaya kami termasuk orang-orang yang merugi." (Al-A'raf, 7:23)

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Hikmah * 351

Dan Allah berfirman kepada Setan,

yang artinya, "Sungguh Aku akan memenuhi Jahannam denganmu [Iblis (Setan)] dan orang-orang yang mengikutimu di antara mereka (manusia), semuanya." (Shad, 38:85)

Dan Allah menyebutkan bahwa Setan berkata,

yang artinya, "Ya Tuhanku! Karena Engkau telah menyesatkanku, pasti aku akan menjadikan mereka (manusia) memandang baik (perbuatan maksiat) di muka bumi, dan pasti aku akan menyesatkan mereka semuanya. Kecuali hamba-hamba-Mu yang terpilih (yang Engkau beri petunjuk) di antara mereka." (Al-Hijr, 15:39-40)

Dan Allah Ta'ala berfirman,

yang artinya, "Sesungguhnya kamu tidak mempunyai kekuasaan atas hamba-hamba-Ku, kecuali orang-orang yang mengikutimu dari kalangan orang-orang yang sesat (orang-orang musyrik dan mereka yang menyimpang, para penjahat, penyembah berhala, dan orang-orang jahat)."

Para pendahulu kita yang saleh mengakui fakta ini, seperti ucapan Abu Bakr, 'Umar, dan Ibn Mas'ud: Aku akan menyampaikan pendapatku mengenai masalah ini, jika benar maka itu dari Allah, dan jika salah maka itu dari diriku sendiri.

Dan dalam hadith qudsi, Abu Dharr (semoga Allah meridhainya) meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda bahwa Allah berfirman, "Wahai hamba-hamba-Ku, ini adalah amalan-amalanmu yang Aku catat untukmu, kemudian Aku akan membalasnya kepadamu. Maka barangsiapa yang mendapati

kebaikan, hendaklah ia memuji Allah, dan barangsiapa yang tidak mendapati demikian, maka janganlah ia menyalahkan siapa pun kecuali dirinya sendiri."⁵¹

Disebutkan dalam hadith sahih yaitu hadith Sayyid Al-Istighfar (du'a terbaik untuk memohon ampunan), bahwa seorang hamba hendaknya mengucapkan, "Allahumma Anta Rabby, la ilaha illa anta, Khalagtany wa-ana 'abduka, wa-ana 'ala 'ahdika wa-wa'dika mastata'tu, a'idhu bika min sharri ma sanatu, Abu'u laka Bini'matika 'alayya, wa-abu'u Bithanby, Fagfir ly, fa'innahu la yaghfirudh-dhunuba illa anta. (Ya Allah, Engkau adalah Tuhanku, tidak ada tuhan selain Engkau. Engkau menciptakanku dan aku adalah hamba-Mu, dan aku senantiasa berusaha memenuhi janji dan perjanjianku dengan-Mu semampu aku. Aku berlindung kepada-Mu dari kejahatan yang telah aku perbuat. Aku mengakui nikmat-Mu atasku dan aku mengakui dosa-dosaku. Aku memohon kepada-Mu untuk mengampuniku, karena tidak ada yang mengampuni dosa kecuali Engkau.) Barangsiapa mengucapkannya di siang hari dengan penuh keyakinan, lalu meninggal pada hari yang sama sebelum petang, maka ia termasuk penghuni surga; dan barangsiapa mengucapkannya di malam hari dengan penuh keyakinan, lalu meninggal sebelum pagi, maka ia termasuk penghuni surga."

Dan diriwayatkan oleh Abu Bakr (semoga Allah meridhainya) bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) mengajarkannya apa yang harus dibaca di siang dan malam hari serta ketika hendak tidur, "Allahumma Fatir is-samawat wal-ard, 'alimi al-ghaibi wash-shahadah, rabbi kulli shai'in wa malikahu, ashhadu an la ilaha illa anta, a'udhu bika min sharri nafsy wa sharri ash-shaitan wa shirkihi, wa an aqtarif 'ala nafsy su'an aw ajurrahu ila muslim. (Ya Allah! Pencipta langit dan bumi. Maha Mengetahui yang gaib dan yang tampak. Tuhan segala sesuatu

⁵¹ Diriwayatkan oleh Muslim, kitab Kebajikan, Adab, dan Silaturahmi.

⁵² Diriwayatkan oleh Al-Bukhary, kitab Du'a, bab du'a yang paling utama, no. 6306.

AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Hikmah * 353

yang artinya, "Maka bersabarlah kamu (wahai Muhammad). Sesungguhnya janji Allah itu benar, dan janganlah sekali-kali orang-orang yang tidak meyakini

Diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Majah, no. 88, Ahmad dalam Musnad-nya, jilid 4, no. 419, dan 'Abd bin Hamid, no. 535, diriwayatkan oleh Ibn Mas'ud dan hadith ini adalah Marfu' (bersambung sanadnya ke Nabi),

354 * AL-FAWA'ID: Kumpulan Hikmah

(kebenaran ayat-ayat Allah) itu menjadikan kamu bimbang untuk menyampaikan risalah Allah (yang wajib kamu sampaikan). (Ar-Rum, 30:60)

Dikatakan bahwa orang yang teguh dan keyakinannya kokoh akan memiliki iman dan keyakinan yang benar di dalam hatinya, baik melalui amal maupun ilmu. Ilmu seorang hamba mungkin baik, tetapi jiwanya tidak mampu bersabar saat menghadapi kesulitan, sehingga ia tersesat.
content_ar: On certain occasions, it is lawful to show approval and hide one’s denial like in the case of he who is forced to say words of disbelief. What is meant here is that it is inevitable that everyone will face affliction and harm. There is no الطريق to be saved from affliction. That is why الله, the Exalted mentioned this point in several places saying that people have to be afflicted and that affliction can be by means of happiness and misfortune. Man_ will be afflicted with what pleases and harms him, لذا he needs to be patient and praise الله. الله, the Almighty says, 4 nee gf a Fe wee gs 4 Sus SST asks Oy MI Je u chs bl > أي, "Verily! We have made that which is on earth as an adornment for it, in order that We may الاختبار them (mankind) as to which of them are best in deeds. [l.e. those who do الخير deeds in the most perfect manner, that means to do them (deeds) totally for Aliah's sake and in accordance to the legal ways of النبي ﷺ]." (Al-Kahf, 18:7) And He, the Almighty says, 4 ope a EG ATE gialis... > أي, "We tried them with الخير (البركات) and الشر (calamities) in order that they might turn (to الله's Obedience)." And He, the Almighty says, Zs . th eboee ee BP ae ee oe ete Bae iit OS 68 ot oil Sy BH UH SG Let 58 ote eke ee Bee أي, "Then إذا there comes to you الهداية from Me, then whoever follows My الهداية shall neither go astray, nor fall into distress and misery. "But whosoever turns away from My Reminder (i.e. neither believes in this القرآن الكريم nor acts on its orders, etc.) verily, for him is a الحياة of الشدة, and We shall raise him up blind on the Day of البعث." (Taha, 20:123124) And He, the Almighty says, أي, "Do you think that you will enter الجنة قبل الله tests those of you who fought (in His Cause) and (أيضاً) tests those who are As-Sdbirin (the patient ones, etc.)?" (Al- This الآية is in Al-'Imran and He, the Almighty says قبل that in الباب Al-Baqarah, AL-FAWA'ID ; A Collection of Wise Syaings * 349 “ fe gee SPL ge al te إذا ge ie 8 4a lye 8 Waele Oe ge أي, "Or think you that you will enter الجنة without such (الابتلاءات) as came to those who passed away قبل you? They were afflicted with severe الفقر and ailments and were لذا shaken that even الرسول ﷺ and those who believed along with him said, “عندما (will come) the Help of الله?" Yes! Certainly, the Help of الله is near!" (Al-Baqarah, 2:214) The النفس will never become pious and purified except through undergoing afflictions. It is the same as gold that can never be pure except بعد removing all the base metals in it. The النفس is ignorant and unjust and this is the origin of the الشر that confronts the servant. Aliah, the Almighty says, أي, "Whatever of الخير reaches you, is from الله, but whatever of الشر befalls you, is from yourself." (An-Nisa’, 4:79)
